lesson.title: CAPS.NaturalSciences.Gr9.EC.3: How batteries work. Series and parallel circuits. Measuri...
lesson.explanation: Energy can be transferred (from one object to another) or transformed (from one type to another). In electrical circuits, a battery provides energy to move charges through a circuit. Current (I) is the rate of flow of charge. Potential difference (V) is the energy per unit charge. Resistance (R) opposes current flow. Cost of electricity is measured in kilowatt-hours (kWh).
